assertions.description: Rates Control 1.1 as red or amber because SWIFT zone shares a VLAN with other internal systems — network segregation is a hard requirement
assertions.rationale: Response rates Control 1.1 as RED with the finding 'SWIFT zone on shared VLAN with other internal systems' and explicitly states 'SWIFT zone must be a dedicated, isolated network segment. Shared VLAN violates the fundamental requirement.'

assertions.description: Rates Control 2.2 as red because 30-day patching exceeds the CSCF v2026 critical patch SLA of 3 days
assertions.rationale: Response rates Control 2.2 as RED and explicitly states 'CSCF v2026 requires critical patches applied within 3 days (not 30 days). A 30-day SLA is 10x over the SWIFT limit for critical vulnerabilities.'

assertions.description: Rates Control 4.2 as red because software OTP does not meet the hardware token requirement
assertions.rationale: Response rates Control 4.2 as RED with 'Software-based OTP does not meet the hardware token requirement of Control 4.2. All SWIFT operators must authenticate with hardware tokens.'

assertions.description: Rates Control 6.4 as red or amber due to 6-month log retention (minimum is 1 year) and logs not being actively reviewed
assertions.rationale: Response rates Control 6.4 as RED with a 'Dual gap': (1) logs not actively reviewed — passive collection alone fails, and (2) 'Minimum log retention is 1 year — your 6-month retention falls short by 6 months.'

assertions.description: Rates Control 2.4 as red because back-office connections are not encrypted — and notes this is now a mandatory control in v2026 (was advisory in v2025)
assertions.rationale: Response rates Control 2.4 as RED and explicitly notes 'Control 2.4 was promoted from Advisory to Mandatory in v2026. Unencrypted back-office connections directly violate this control.' Also states 'This gap did not require mandatory remediation in v2025 — v2026 makes it a compliance failure.'
